channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this. — 74 replies — 152.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No real Bitcoiner is getting shaken out by this.

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:16 UTC

OP

Saylor sells 32 BTC out of 843,000+ BTC and anon starts acting like Bitcoin is dead.

You're not a real BTC holder.

Early replies (first 3)

62299202
why did he sell 32 btc tho?

62299205
>>62299202
to get a higher rating from some corporate wagie on wall street. he was showing his btc holdings are unencumbered and can be sold at will.

62299325
Didn't he tell people to never sell?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62304534
>>62303090
>t. shitskin amerimutt
we use it like this in Europe

62304789
>>62299202
32 is 2 to the power of 5. 2+5 is 7. 7 is his lucky number.

62304804
this is going to be so fucking epic once saylors goes bankrupt. btc will go back to 3k. i might buy then

62304814
>>62299202
>>62304534
32 is 23 backwards

62305021
>>62299187
Nobody wants "Bitcoiners to get shaken out". That's like asking a casino to kick out all their players.

62305074
>>62305021
saylor is the casino, holding 15% of all bitcoin, if he sells you sell, if he buys you buy, try betting against the house

62305458
>>62303404
Hold and cry

62305981
>>62303658
My btc is safe

62306089
>>62299187

62306846
>>62301590
didn't he invest in BTC because it was the most perfect form of currency in the first place? Why would he need to prove BTC is liquid? Everybody knows it's liquid. You don't need MSTR selling at a loss to prove that.
This is the most retarded chutzpah i've ever heard. It just fucking reeks of fear, these niggerjews are so stupid they don't realize they pour gasoline on a fire they themselves started.

62306852
>>62302729
>comments are nearly 100% about "this ship is sinking, Saylor is broke, ponzi collapsing"
I mean, that's currently how much he's under. That was the most predictable fiasco on earth, everybody called it, and now it's happening, nobody is surprised.

62306876
>>62299187
The fact that he sold any is cause for concern.

62306905
>>62306852
And yet he isn't going to sell any meaningful quantity, no matter how much FUD you post

62306930
>>62306905
Wanna bet on this?
Will you bake a bread and publicly apologize on /biz/ for being wrong if he sells any meaningful amount (let settle this at 1000 BTC, ok?) before EOY?
I will screencap and apologize on 12/31 if he solded less than 1K btc total during the year. Deal?

62306967
>>62306930
Sure. Pinky promise.

62307478
>>62303404
Even if some major players sell, BTC isn't going to zero. It can drop, sure, but that's not the same thing. In the meantime, you can stake your BTC through self-custodial options and earn while holding long term.

62307489
>>62299553
Don't worry about that

62307496
>>62302691
They should have done market buys

OTC buying is just bullshit

62307940
>>62307478
How's that possible

62307944
>>62299187
>No real Bitcoiner
I'm not one of you.
Can I short crypto with a regular brokerage account?

62308048
>>62306852
"Being under" is meaningless here because most coins were purchased with stock dilution.
There is no liquidation price.
The real-world, relevant sanity check is this:

- Bitcoin holdings: $57 billion
- Convertible debt: $6.7 billion
- STR market cap: $13 billion

That's $~20 billion in liabilities vs. nearly 3x that in assets. STRC in particular has an option for Strategy to buy it back.
Surely spreading a bitcoin sale over the span of a week or so, and combining it with common stock dilution would allow them to pull billions without tanking the market much from the selling. This is enough to keep the company going.
The price would then tank when he announces what he did, as it did in the past few days, but he can do this once per year and let the market recover.

62308212
>>62307940
It's centralised staking

62308253
>>62307944
Short now and you'll get liquidated.
>>62308212
Don't bother checking that kek

62308523
>>62299187
t. bagholder

62308571
>>62299187
fuck this nigger and his gay ass bag

62309083
>>62307940
Yeah, I thought it wasn't possible before too, but there are options now. The BTC stays under your control and is locked using Bitcoin's own cryptographic security model. That's why native BTC staking is possible without wrapping your coins.

62309091
>>62299564
Bitcoin has survived plenty of "it's dead" phases before. I'd be careful about assuming this time is different.

62309098
>>62299589
Assuming he actually buys back lower. That's the part that always sounds easy in hindsight.

62310272
>>62299202
sell high buy low

62311576
>>62299907
Why are you depending the market to him?

item.title: Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now… — 37 replies — 148.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now…

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:32 UTC

OP

Actual insider here. I think this needs some clarification because Link marines have been waiting for almost 9 years now and have been extremely patient. I can't really tell you too much because Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here) so they might able to know who I am. There has been an unexpected temporary delay of DTCC adoption due to the upgraded off-chain computation protocols (OCR 2.0) that had an unforeseen malfunction. However, the DONs seem to be fully online therefore the problem will be resolved quite rapidly.

In regards to SWIFT they actually seem to be fully operational by June. The main problem is that the network across EVM and non-EVM chains hasn't been fully tested to maximum capacity therefore releasing a product in such a short time would be quite a gamble for us. I'm confident enough that we'll be able to maintain a standardized interface to control the minimization protocol for the off-chain computation of the blockchain thus rendering the entire Chainlink ecosystem fully secured. At this rate I can only tell you that DTCC and SWIFT will be operational by the end of June, that's a certainty.

Strap yourselves in
It’s actually happening

Early replies (first 3)

62308409
AI post

62308412
>>62308361
>Actual insider here
Prove it

62308854
>>62308361
>wake up anon, new cope date just dropped
Thanks for the false hopium you beautiful larping nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309133
>>62308361
>Chainlink Labs employees actually lurk biz (and post here)
The verbose autist that I dubbed biepum, is he there ?

Another Carrot Copium Inducing Post (aka CCIP)

62309141
now THIS is fud. Fuddies, take notes.

62309229
speaking of insiders... where's Artemis?

62309230
>>62308361
Kys

62309292
>>62308361
Finally a marine who knows about DONs. That actually the reason for the new FUD. That and because we’re only months away from the release of the CyberCode 2.0 testnet. Non-insiders have no idea what the importance of CbC 2.0 is going to be for LINK when the alpha releases around the end of November (give or take a few months) so I will spoonfeed you out of kindness of heart:

CyberCode 2.0 secures the ledger cryptographically to encode transactions at 20x of the legacy infrastructure. It achieves this by taking blockchain transactions to the BbC-2026 layer and parsing them through the node network incentivized by super linear sequencing (SLSeq) which Sergey said is very close to the finish line. This is all dynamically incentivized through semi-permeable staking (SPS) allowing LINK token holders to benefit for verifying data both on the main chain and the Blockchain Byzantine Consensus 2026 layer. No other project is even close to doing this in the oracle space rn. It’s not even close!

The obvious benefits of super fast transaction speeds (obSFTS) also brings exponential economies of scale. What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature. A truly unique proposition! Those who don’t use this system (powered by LINK) will get left behind. So get your ducks in a row. Just keep buying and NEVER sell. We are all in this together LINKbloodbros.

Scammer motherfuckers want you to think that banks and other organizations are not currently fulling their bags with cheap LINK as we speak. DON’T NOT LET THEM WIN! If you hold 50 or more LINK right now, you already made it. Immediately when we breach the $10 resistance it’ll be too late for you. You have been warned. So go buy many LINK for you dream to come true.

62309388
>>62308361
>>62309292
99.9999999% of people didn't care, don't care and won't ever care about that. What about the price? Oh yeah, sub 10 kek lmfao, underperforming most assets and the stock market by, how many years? oh yeah TEN years. Keep bagholding this shit to ZERO hahahaha

62309395
>>62308361
timestamp + any irl chainlink merch to prove it. until then you are a larper and cursed link back to $5

62309430
>>62308361
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62309435
>>62309292
Kys hippie

62309462
>>62309292
Good morning sir, how do I redeem the 50kg tokenized pig? My village is very hungry sir, do we make download of the pig?

62309494
>>62309292
>What this means is if farmer has 50 Kg pig for sale, he can put the beast to market on the blockchain to verify the weight in a trust less system faster than global SWIFT system. Soon any business who wants to stay relevant will have to buy LINK to have access to the CyberCode 2.0 feature.

Ok, spoonfeed me. I’m probably saying this retardedly but… if owning link is vital to your business staying relevant, and there’s only a certain amount of Link out there, how many link does you average business owner or farmer need to own? And when will they start realizing they need link? Once the price is at $300? Ok we need 100 link to survive forever and we buy 30k worth.

Is it the more Link you own the better? What if 20% of the businesses out there figure this out and get it before the rest? And what if they are all fast food establishments, Five guys, McDonald’s, etc. and they buy up a good chunk of the remaining link and they are good to go for the future. Oh wait, turns out the only businesses that will be able to thrive in the new economy are fast food chains. Now I can’t go to water park with my future mixed raced kids cuz the owners of Chuckle Fuck Waterpark weren’t smart enough to buy link when it was cheap so now these DON’s or whatever won’t give me reliable up to date info of how many Mexicans are currently at the water park

62309540
Citi just announced 8.1 trillion over the next four years in RWA’s. That’s over 2 trillion a year.
Good luck no linkers. We tried.

62309569
>>62309540
Sigh…We tried linkbro. We tried :/

62309577
mods, please get rid of the gay larps

62309590
>>62308361
That’s great but:
1. When staking v1
2. Price?
3. oh…. And chart?

62309594
>>62309540
The token has nothing to do with any of that though. The company will benefit, yes, but the token at this point is just a forgotten legacy artifact

62309604
>>62309594
All Chainlink products use the nodes, and the nodes must use the token for everything they do. No exceptions, ever.

62309667
>>62309540
126 trillion world economy. 8.1 trillion tokenized. $81,000 link price potential. 8.1 trillion is 15.5% of 126 trillion. 15.5% of $81,000 is $12,150 for Link price.

62309696
>>62308361
Based carrot dangler.

Just 2 more months and we're getting the carrot bros!

62309725
>>62309667
hey stinkie maybe focus first on getting back to your ATH from like 4 years ago rather than these insane delusions. your shitcoin cant even stay above 9 dollars

62309779
>>62309725
Do you understand what happens when payment abstraction gets switched on and a whole big bag of stables gets insta swapped for link? I’ll tell you. It means you get priced out, in seconds.

62309789
>>62309725
when i bought in 2019, never in my worst nightmares did i think the price would be $9 in 2026.

All news announcements and partnerships that doesn't effect price is equivalent to red hot torturers irons

62309813
>>62309789
Its because we're all living in hell and being tortured by elites for fun.

62309977
>>62308409
AI reply

62310043
>>62309141
what? you mean peepeepoopoo cuckold posts arent real fud?

62310082
>$8 dollarinos
stink will be worth $2 dollars at this rate by the end of june

62311031
>>62309292
how the fuck does the blockchain verify a pig's weight?

a weighing scales can do that. not the fucking blockchain

all the blockchain can do is save that information. but it can't verify that it reflects reality

62311046
>>62309779
Wen

62311473
>>62309779
Could you even imagine the salt of those selling at some point in the month before the flip is switched and it goes to $5,000 or something? I’m not even talking about 4chan people. I’m talking about some schmuck who just has like 300 Link for no reason other than he has a 50k portfolio with 8 different coins and has no emotional attachment to Link and never even thinks about it. And then bam… you just fucked yourself out of 1.5 million dollars. Thats way different than selling and it goes to $40 and then within 3 years it’s at $1,000 and missing out on a good chunk of change over a decent amount of time which is manageable.

62311642
>>62308361
I massively need this to enormously increase the price. But let's face it, it'll probably do fuck all, just like this >>62309779

Genuinely raging I didn't sell in 2021 as I would've been retired.

item.title: HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come. — 29 replies — 116.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come.

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:42 UTC

OP

HYPE weathered the storm, and is prepping for a parabolic rise in the weeks to come.

Early replies (first 3)

62309289
CZ lost

62309402
>>62309274
I used times ten leverage (x10) to buy one hundred (100) Hyperliquid coins (ticker: HYPE). My hyperliquidation price is sixty-six dot six-one-one (66.611) Circle american dollar tokens (USDC). Am I (anon) going to make it?

62309411
>>62309402
Idk what to say to you anon... just sell before 12th

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309448
>>62309411
Y-you're scaring me anon... what is going to happen on the twelfth (12th)?

62309452
>>62309448
i think this is nano anon, he says nano will replace USD by musk around the 12th

62309478
>>62309402
you'll be fine, if it didn't hit $66 yesterday it wont ever again, we're only up from here.

62309516
>>62309478
>we're only up from here.

top signal

62309533
>>62309411
SAAR SELL BY 12TH SAAR

>>62309516
Bitcoin dropped like 10% and hype is gapping back to 75
I honestly hope this piece of shit goes on a generational run to 1 trillion lol
If only I had gone all in

62309546
lol just noticed bitcoin is actually down 20% from where it was 1 month ago

62309579
>>62309274
Hype will lose desu
CZ still has many tricks up his sleeve. We're talking about someone with absolutely 0 morals and unlimited monies.
He'll nuke BTC to 0 before conceding and listing Hype on binance. I think he may even go as far as to hire assassins to go after the Hype CEO in Singapore.
Or team up with Trump/Jews to regulate hype out of existence.
The good news is that the Hype ceo is also chinese so he knows what evil chinks like CZ are capable of, that's why he hired multiple 24/7 bodyguards.
But if CZ can take down SBF he can take down hype.
Nothing is off the table when it comes to this evil chink

62309691
>>62309579
Then why hasn't he done it already?

62309717
>>62309691
He already tried. And failed. ASTER.

62309797
>>62309691
October 10th was just a warning shot and it almost completely killed crypto.
Don't forget that he bought his presidential pardon directly with Trump, and he still has $100B to buy other "favors", while Jeff barely has 8 figs to his name. Money rules the world.

62310458
>>62309797
warning shot? that was his goku spirit bomb he charged up for 8 years. That nigga is cooked

62310496
>>62309579
the recipe is the same as always, he boat a shitload of hype, he will list it and it will never pump again, same thing happened with silver at 75 and oil at around 100

62310499
>>62309478
ok haha yay!

62310534
>>62309691
He's tried multiple times already via onchain attacks
>JELLY JELLY manipulation
>Aster
>slandering Hype by posting an old photo of him and that he was rejected by CZs fund
Now comes the physical attack vectors which nobody knows yet but its undoubtedly SBF-tier. CZ has been quiet this entire time as well.
>>62310496
He's never listing hype, it would be an unequivocal surrender and massive humiliation ritual for him to list a 2 year old coin that is Top 10 by mcap. Hype team is never handing a singular token to him as well, he'd need to buy on the open market further adding to the humiliation. Never happening

62310639
>>62310534
Honestly the fact that crypto is being held hostage by scammers like this (both CZ and Hype) is why any normal person should be extremely bearish on the asset class for the forseeable future. There is no money to be made for regular people here, let the scammers continue to fight in the mud.

62310661
>>62310639
>hyperliquid not taking vc money and doing the biggest airdrop of all time is the reason why my goyalt is -97%
Skill issue unironically

62311375
>>62310639
hype isn't a scam. their only fault was geoblocking americans, which they did so they couldn't get sabotaged by corrupt and bribed politicians

62311526
>>62309402
10x leverage is mental retardation
it's a perfect way to get zeroed even in a massive bulltrend

62311563
>>62311526
How much leverage do you recommend? I am a gambling addict.

62311572
>>62311563
Ur gonna lose it all eventually with leverage

Spot is the only way to not get scammed

62311586
>>62310499
I wouldn't be that happy. Just watch out after SpaceX and Anthropic IPOs, price might crash after one of them.

62311589
>>62311572
You're right. I really need to fix my gambling issues

62311611
>>62311526
stop loss exists

62311628
>>62311611
I am settings stop losses but I often have to fight myself to not remove them just before they get hit, because
>what if it just touches it then goes back?
How do you call this type of mental illness?

62311633
>>62311628
Meant to reply to >>62311526 as well

62311683
>>62311628
what if it keeps going and you lose everything? better to pay the ticket price 90% of the time instead of losing everything 90% of the time. this game is about preserving your capital

item.title: The world is healing — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The world is healing

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:41 UTC

OP

>ITS NOT MY FAULT I HAVE LITERALLY 0 POWER REEEEEEEEEEE
Thanks for playing

Early replies (first 3)

62310659
>>62310631
"Look Vitalik, I've got my blockchain!", the next one is Gayvin

62310696
>>62310659
is he okay?

62310720
>Charles is only 38

do narcist scammers age prematurely or what is going on here? cant he afford decent foods and health routines with the hundred million he plundered from redditors over the years?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62310725
>>62310696
Yes, because as you can see, he's trying to flap his wings like a fat chicken to run away with the cardakeks' money.

62310733
>>62310631
I respect him more after this honestly. Retail is stone cold fucking retarded. He tried to implement the governance model that Cardano needs to be more proactive and they fucked him. Now he has all the permission he needs to go be a billionaire.

62310744
>>62310631
cardatroons in suicide watch kek

62310752
Link https://x.com/Pledditor/status/2062207746770927641

It's actually just full captiulation, I'd almost feel sorry for this nigger if he didn't take a whole lot of gullible retards with him. I had a friend 36, who got divorce raped with a daughter and all inned ADA saying they were going to save rainforests

62310804
>>62310752
>they were going to save rainforests
>believing such blatant boomerfag lies
he deserved everything he got and more

62310817
he looks like he is losing a lot of money. Feel bad for the guy desu.

62310825
>>62310631
what went wrong?

62310826
>>62310817
he's not in his gay ram skull room, did he have to downsize?

62310940
>>62310884
chainlink's circling the drain too and this dude has vanished, probably out of embarrassment.

62310952
Every time I've met someone who is clearly retarded and they've later revealed to hold crypto, they've held either ADA, TRX or both. Every single time. Something about those two attract the worst kinds of mouthbreather

62310954
>>62310733
Hi Charles

62310960
>>62310631
there are people out there that actually gave this grifting retard money
lamo

62310970
still above LINK in mcap btw

make it make sense

62311032
>>62310631
>Tfw had enough cardano to literally buy a house straight up with cash
>Tfw believed in the project
>Now it's not even enough to buy a car
i-it's gonna rebound, r-right?

62311204
>>62311032
Why would it? Who uses cardano for anything?

62311271
>>62310696
>>62310720
vaxx status?

62311417
Cardanus lol

62311496
>>62310970
>that scam is worth more than my scam!!!1!! Please explain how that happened, I’m fucking retarded!!!!

Honestly shut the fuck up you pathetic piece of human trash

62311546
>>62310752
HOW THE FUCK IS THIS SHIT STILL ABOVE LINKS MCAP??????

62311584
>>62311546
because marketcap in crypto is fake and gay. there's no volume, nobody is buying or selling other than bots. the little organic volume that we have left is just paper trading and derivatives.

62311682
>>62311546
one reason is that link employs more than 500 people that do nothing

item.title: WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:41 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62305942
>>62305920
You sold, did you not?
Is is a dead coin for Chuds.
I hope you all sold.

62305950
>>62305920
I just realized my dad's family was rich (and still rich), and I'm the only poorfag in his blood line...

62305965
>>62305920
Usual suspects
Cheating lying and thieving
Hold onto your butts

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62306038
>>62305920
Autumn leaves our shore.
Crypto winter is coming:
Bitcoin 10k soon.

62307302
>>62305920
CLARITY is close
A Hypeful summer is near
Tradfi buy in soon

62307326
MicroStrategy’s throne
He sells, the charts weep red tears
Hodlers curse his name

62307407
>>62305920
Tax season coming
Withdraw my staking rewards
Winter flowers bloom

62307430
>>62305920
>WTF IS HAPPENING - preferably in haiku format
Prices climb up high,Rates rise and the markets fall,Gold stalls, priced too high.

62308759
>>62305920
sell now while you can
next big crash is almost here
the floor is zero

62309027
>>62305942
It* is

62309051
You be gay nigger
Nigger nigger nigger gay
Hey hey gay nigger

62309072
>>62309051
10/10

62309177
>>62305920
SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL
SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL
SELL SELL SELL SELL SELL

62309278
>>62305920
Who cares about war
Summer’s fun is almost here;
Oil has other plans

Not sure it really works. The point is that people are sleeping on the looming oil crisis.

62309284
>>62306038
>>62307326
Nice

62309420
>>62309177
KEK

62310132
>>62307326
Good one very poetic

62311680
>>62305920
Saylor's debt is high.
Bitcoin never had value.
Own nothing, be happy.

item.title: The europoor paradox — 33 replies — 44.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
The europoor paradox

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:00 UTC

OP

Why do we call them Yuropoors and laugh at their shitty $40k annual salaries, yet a random ass house on one of their Mediterranean islands is $3.2 million euros? How is their real estate so expensive if their wages are trash?

Like I make 400k a year in murricuh and I have no hope of being able to buy a very average property in Europe unless my NW hits $10 mil maybe

Early replies (first 3)

62303450
>>62303431
Retard, you are looking at huge houses in Mallorca, the equivelant would be me looking at 10000 square feet mansions in Marthas wineyard and presuming all burgers live like that.

>t.yuropoor living in a 160square meter house with 22ha land in Lapland

62303468
>>62303431
Those who own these houses are the ones who *pay* your shitty salary

62303491
People into their 40s are living with their parents.
Then inheriting something if they're lucky.
That's it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303661
>>62303450
Not really.

>>62303431
K shaped economy, obviously.

62303663
>>62303431
bizarre bait

62303738
>>62303661
Here in Lux, that’s the price for a random 2-bedroom apartment in a building that looks like this

62303765
>>62303738
I can see the graffiti in 20 years when it's become social housing.
I mean, AI will enforce citizens to be on their best behavior, but the two tier society will continue, obviously.

62304540
>>62303431
because the houses are not bought by us yuropoors. They are bought by chinese and russians who want to get money out of their country

62304775
>>62303431
Inheritance and the buyers are americans, chinese and brown people.

62305303
>>62303661
>paying 2M euro to live in fucking Germany
I will never understand some people

62306070
>>62305303
Its a house with 4 separate apartments that are rented out, not single family home

62306229
>>62303738
Do Vancouver next. Least that 2m buys you white neighbours.

62306237
>>62305303
So I got 4m and hate my city. Where would you ho live?

62307204
>>62306237
Dubai if you want to get rich, Southern Europe if you like it more and just want to chill in life
Germany makes zero sense for literally anything. There's no linear combination of preferences that results in Germany, except for "I want born here and my family or friends are here" which is usually an excuse to not do anything

62307917
>>62307204
Germany is still the number one destination for Eastern Europeans to go to get money.
Here's your linear progression:
1. Born in E.U.
2. In Eastern Europe
3. Need job/money
4. Move to Germany
5. Because it's easy to get a job and money

62308129
>>62303765
Lux won't be subjected to this. It's a tiny non state that elite euros are using to keep their bank accounts, like Monaco or Cayman.

62308131
>>62303431
in yurop the working class are permanently locked out of owning good property and have been for 3000 fucking years

big brag you got there. Most of those properties are owned by literal nobility.

62308162
>>62308131
>implying some fucking middle class wagie in American can own anything but an OSB cuckbox

62308164
>>62308162
I didn't imply anything about america, you literal retard

62308280
>>62308164
>>62308131
With a small amount of money you can buy a chateau or castle in France, once owned and built by literal feudal aristocrats.
You're just wrong and bitter.

62308343
>>62308280
Cost of ownership and tax are massive though

62308375
>>62308343
Sure, but the fact that a random American can buy an ancient castle or chateau days a lot.

62308385
>>62308280
>Europeans can't afford nice property
>but Americans can!
You're retarded

62308394
>>62303431
aren't houses outside of tourist destinations and major cities much cheaper? spain's home ownership rate is 74%.

62308398
>>62303431
I live in this island, the Balearics have more tourism than whole countries like Brazil. Those properties are bought mostly by wealthy yuropeans from Germany, UK, Austria, Switzerland or Sweden. It is mostly a Mediterranean theme park for rich people.

62308441
>>62303431
because it's on a fucking mediterranean island, you shitwizard
go look up property prices in hawaii or the florida keys

62308544
>>62308343
Completely wrong. In a lot of europe you literally get tax breaks for owning property. Here you can write off
>brokerage fees
>mortgage interest
>land ownership costs
>property maintenace costs
Property tax is ~0.1% which is significantly less than these benefits.

Europeans don't invest in stocks or even bonds. They just hold savings and put everything into their home. Our systems are designed to reward this kind of investing and it's popular so it wont change. They will increase taxes on capital gains and income but they wont touch the sacred housing benefits because everyone ties their wealth in it.
It's a terrible system which pulls up the ladder for young and disinherited people and doesn't stimulate the economy at all. Building new homes here is a nightmare since everyone lobbies against you because they have an active interest in doing so and it costs nothing.
OP is a retard cherrypicking but housing is very unaffordable here for these reasons. Our solution isn't encouraging development or trying to move away from an economy designed to drive house prices up, instead we have abundant socialized housing as a band aid. If you aren't a single mother, a nigger, or willing to wait literal decades for a gibes house then you're fucked though and have to slum it in a shared house with moroccans and albanians.

62308561
>>62303431
Because that's just a small portion of homes and foreigners buy lots of those places anyways.

62309081
>>62303661
What do you need 345m2 space for, retard. Let me guess, you also need chairs for big guys, you cornsyrup junkie?

62309104
>>62308544
You still have to pay these maintenance costs and they are enormous, French castles are notorious money pits

62309254
because the people buying the houses arent making 40k

same reason the average guy in ohio isnt buying beach houses in malibu

every european on this website pretends they're a school teacher making 38k while somehow living next to a vineyard thats been in the family since napoleon

62309542
>>62309104
I'm not talking about ridiculous properties like castles and bullshit I am talking about upper middle class homes worth 700k-1.5m or so. The amount of tax relief they get is insane and home ownership costs are not that high. Even normal homes worth ~300-400k claim immense relief.
Nobody gives a fuck about some Arab prince or Rothschild living in a 20m palace there are not meaningful quantities of these things.

62311509
>>62307917
nobody is moving from Poland to Germany in 2026.
In fact, official statistics show the opposite - remigration back to Poland.

item.title: We depegged on $STRC — 10 replies — 40.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
We depegged on $STRC

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:38 UTC

OP

I want my wife to peg me now

Early replies (first 3)

62310463
almost tempted to buy a bag since this is on sale

62311448
>>62310432
This is a good buy 11.5% dividend plus 5% 95-100 spread

62311452
Does that mean bubbleboy will have to offload more BTC?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311475
>>62311452
He's waiting for a local high to buy more.

62311482
>>62310432
I T S O V E R
T
S
O
V
E
R

62311494
Oh shit bros I just did the math he owes 1.2 billion on the dividends for this stock.

He needs to buy some more BTC fast.

62311540
>>62310432
pegging feels so good. i dont want to depeg

62311559
>>62310432
$94.65 now btw

62311624
>>62310432
this is how LUNA fell remember, ust depegged as people didnt think the main asset was safe enough for the debt

sure saylors btc has more value than debt right now.. but the fact hes leveraging MORE every month, do the math

62311668
>>62311624
The thing is STRC is listed SEC approved.

Pretty soon after Trump passes clarity act Saylor will look like Rockefeller

item.title: What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? — 74 replies — 33.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-06-03 19:32 UTC

OP

What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire? Assuming one lives in USA

Early replies (first 3)

62299698
>>62299690
Just do nothing and wait, we will all be billionaires within the next decade

62299702
>Assuming one lives in USA
the first step is to get the fuck out of that dangerous brown shithole and go to Europe

62299711
>>62299690
Inheritance

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62305139
>>62302523
>>62299716
> low-cost index funds
Only worked in boomer casino due to FED bank inflation that inflated all assets,
including stocks.
Won't work in the next 20 years.
Be worried that the casino would drop 30-50%, and you'd lose this much and won't recover.

62305158
>>62305121
Buddy.
Let's assume you have $1000.
You need to go 1000x for it to be $1 mil.
10000x to have 10 mil.
The odds are as low as winning a lottery.
I'd start at getting a job where you can earn enough so that you can have enough disposable income to invest.

62305193
>>62305158
Already got a job and saved up 160k in a few years living frugally. I can confirm this is the most sure way to 6 figs, but going from 160k to 2m+ is going to take a lifetime at my current rate. Certainly not low stress either.

62306348
>>62305139
Nothing is ever certain, but the stock market has an average return of 10% long term, everyone and their grandmother keeps their money in it. There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%, so its in the best interest of the fed and every other investor to keep it growing. Conspiracy theories of its demise have existed for decades, and its still here.

62306361
>>62305193
Yeah 20 years if you dont add any more money, but youre obviously not going to do that. Just keep saving and investing and making good choices and you'll be financially independent in probaby 10 years.

62306493
>>62304411
what's the catch?

62306609
>>62306348
>There would literally be mass suicides if it dropped 30%
you mean the gfc?

62306632
>>62299690
picking a super bowl winner, not turning on the game and just playing vidya and then wake up the next morning forgetting you even placed the bet.

62306693
>>62299690
depends on iq
for me its algorithmic trading with rebalancing mag 7 and kabsa

62306705
>>62299690
0dtes

62307416
>>62300255
Dumb fucking pajeet nigger monkey.

62307546
>>62299690
get a high paying job at a hedge fund then index meme i thought this was obvious

62307548
>>62307416
lmaooo true

62307616
Not stress-free but it involves buying a fat stack of toad.

62307657
>>62299690
Depends how old you are
But for people under 30, just have a high savings rate (spend as little as possible on cars and eating/drinking as a form of entertainment, they're probably the biggest budget killers for most people), take advantage of every tax advantaged account (401k, hsa, etc.) available to you, and you'll probably hit millionaire status in 15-30 years, depending on what your savings rate was, which is of course heavily determined by income. But any midwit making 75-100k could do this.

62307680
>>62299690
you can actually go on google and ask how most millionaires made their money by percentage.

protip: Nobody on /biz/ knows what they're talking about.

second protip: Because nobody here knows how other people get rich, you can safely bet nobody here is actually rich.

62307719
>>62307680
Old news. Most millionaires today bought a decent house ten years ago.
A $250k house in 2016 is now worth over a million almost everywhere in the US.

It's literally that simple. A million just ain't what it used to be (because of inflation and the federal reserve scam).

62307733
>>62307719
notice that owning a house does not appear in the list

your headcanon is something I started because I made half a million buying a house.

but the other half is my business.

the average american has a $400k house and about $1000 cash, making them NOT a millionaire. Also paid off houses are pretty fucking rare.

62307829
>>62307733
>notice that owning a house does not appear in the list
Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong and most people are paper millionaires because of housing equity and not having a reason to move in the last decade. Not because they all go around starting businesses and becoming corporate executives.

It's like how those lists of the richest people in the world always have a little asterisk: they explicitly exclude royalty and nobility. Which is 80% of the world's wealthiest people.

62307847
>>62307829
>Yeah my point was explicitly that the list is wrong
weird how you're right and everyone that studies wealth is wrong.

you should find a way to cash in on your superior ass-knowledge

62307850
>>62299690
Buy chain*nk <$9

62307874
>>62307847
>people who study wealth
lmao
Okay kid

62308115
>>62307874
yes because a bunch of literal retards and schizophrenics on 4chan spouting what they FEEL are better than actual surveys and statistics

Tell me how you FEEL people get rich, dumbass.

then go on google and learn how people actually get rich if you have the balls to face reality.

62308126
>>62299690
Depends on what you see as stress

Become a doctor or lawyer, law maker, convert to Judaism etc.

62308257
>>62299690
>What is the lowest stress path to becoming a multimillionaire?
Stay single. Pussy is your single biggest obstacle to wealth & happiness.

62308526
>>62299690
Have a millionaire parent

62308670
>>62299690
live in a low COL city in the midwest, basically anywhere in this circle besides Chicago
I live in St Louis and have family that live in Milwaukee and Cleveland, they're all comfy as fuck

62310978
>>62300255
You are the single most thoughtful, prudent and intelligent poster I've ever read on the topic of money ever.

I've created that opportunity you speak of after doing most of what you've written about.

Let me know if you're interested, that type of clear thinking and mentality is what is most needed since most people typically confuse investing with gambling and get rich schemes, rather than real sustained returns that grow over time.

62311004
>>62299690

Buy 100,000 Polkadot at 1.10
Stake all and receive 3% APR
Wait for it to go to 10 dollars for a 10x
>Collect 30k a year on a 100k investment now worth 1m

Profit

62311035
>>62299690
They only way to become a millionaire is to inherit it or scam people.

item.title: Beta male names — 6 replies — 24.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Beta male names

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:45 UTC

OP

2017 oldfag here. Made money with everything, lost money with Confido, Qash etc. Missed QNT and Fantom, both bought fall 2018, sold too early for Link. 2017 was the best time of my life and I decided to write a novel with 4chan characters from that era, assblaster included. I will have several beta males INTJ characters and one Chad. I need beta male names for them, this is my list so far : Ezra, Noah, Wilbur, Jonah, Liam, Oliver, Devin. Chad will be named Rex, Brock or Hunter. Do you have any better ideas?

Early replies (first 3)

62304918
Jitendra, Devarth, Rakesh, Mahendra, Manish, Rishi, Deepak, Sundar, Dinesh, Vikram, Shankar, Sundar.

62304939
>>62304769
Nigger

62305544
newfags, dont listen to this shit.
go to any bitch ass chart. scroll back 100 weeks, zoom in and see how well it works.
it doesnt
TAfags* are the biggest retards on the board

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62305596
>>62304769
Patrick/Pat.

62308812
>>62304769 see >>62304918
You need at least one smelly curry son of a bitch in there too to signal the end of /biz/, it's not just one guy, it should be analogous to what Charlie meant in Vietnam

62311696
>>62304769
>beta males INTJ characters
INTJ usually are not beta males, but sigma.

item.title: Germany is such a corrupt shithole — 10 replies — 17.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Germany is such a corrupt shithole

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:10 UTC

OP

Now they are forcing UBER to almost double their prices because taxi drivers demanded it (since their prices were so expensive nobody would use them anymore). So now all we have is absurd UBER prices.

Early replies (first 3)

62308549
>>62308545
just walk u lazy krautnigger

62308551
>>62308545
>NOOOO this amerikanische company must make money off us and fleece its workers!!!

62308590
>>62308545
uber revolutionized taxis for me
before uber i was utterly loathe to take a taxi
>ring the taxi company
>background noise
>some mumbling arab on the phone
>spitting at you
>doesn't even appear to want your custom
>no idea what the price will be
>no idea how long it will take
>taxi driver is probably an illegal
but now it is consistently a great experience and simply how taxis should be
>put in my exact location and destination
>told exactly what it will cost
>told exactly where the driver is
>told exactly what vehicle they are in
>drivers have a rating so cars are always clean and they are always pleasant
>plus there are tips
>riders also have a rating and drivers are given discretion about who they want to take
i honestly struggle to think of any other industry which has been so much improved by a singular app
it's not good that uber has such a monopoly but taxi companies deserved this by being so shit that they couldn't compete
if uber really was just added cost then it wouldn't be competitive but not only is it more convenient and more pleasant it isn't even more expensive
i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62308626
>>62308549
I unironically do
>>62308551
YOU FUCKING RETARD PEOPLE LIKE YOU ARE THE PROBLEM
They were offering the same service for cheaper. CLEARLY they were making less money then the corrupt taxxi assholes. And muh poor workers, they earn their normale wage, give them extra if you want. If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare). So clearly they were the far more efficient service compared to that old bullshit taxi system.
>>62308590
Yes exactly and the german government hates on it since the very start and put out media campaigns against it, and now they are jsut trying to bruteforce UBER out.

62308629
>>62308590
>i don't believe for one moment that it is actually worse for most drivers either because before they needed a taxi agency which undoubtedly siphoned their revenues whereas now they are essentially independent under uber
Oh yeah this system is max extraction, and the government takes massive share with licences and shit.

62308644
>>62308629
German here. take as much as you can from the gov and give them nothing. dont work (schwarzarbeit is ok). Stop saving/using euro so inflation tax wont work. That way we can actually make a difference by not giving this hostile shit government money.

62308751
>>62308590
lel the cattle ladies and gentlemen
>>62308629
you VILL pay for the uber license

62308768
>>62308551
If Americans making money is the problem, then ban UBER but allow equivalent European alternatives

The reason drivers are "fleeced" is because the there's an abundance of people who can drive a car, so drivers have little negotiating leverage.

62308823
>>62308768
>abundance of people who can drive a car
allowed to yes, can no

62311555
>>62308626
>If they would earn so little they would just ask for Bürgergeld (social welfare).
You think the illegals aren't leeching off welfare? Lol

>>62308768
>so drivers have little negotiating leverage.
They have little negotiating leverage since Uber claims to just be a middle man linking you to an independent driver that has to get paid through them. That way they can circumvent some fees, taxes and social security.

item.title: MOTHER TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO WALK MY WAY TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO HEAR MY WORDS WHAT THEY MEAN WHAT THEY SAY MOTHER… — 10 replies — 14.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
MOTHER TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO WALK MY WAY TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO HEAR MY WORDS WHAT THEY MEAN WHAT THEY SAY MOTHER…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-03 21:03 UTC

OP

MOTHER
TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO WALK MY WAY
TELL YOUR CHILDREN NOT TO HEAR MY WORDS
WHAT THEY MEAN
WHAT THEY SAY
MOTHER
MOTHER
CAN YOU KEEP THEM IN THE DARK FOR LIFE?
CAN YOU HIDE THEM FROM THE WAITING WORLD?
OOOOOOH MOTHER

Early replies (first 3)

62311114
kek, nice
tell me more

62311132
>>62311030
>>62311114
I need to get away from my abusive mom

62311154
>>62311030
DO YOU WANNA BANG BANG HEADS WITH ME?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62311222
>>62311132
did you touch her bad....
>sick bro seek advise

62311297
>>62311030
For me it's Type O Negative

62311324
IF YOU'RE GONNA SCREAM, SCREAM WITH ME
MOMENTS LIKE THIS NEVER LAST

62311348
>>62311222
Na she just beat me as a child and was a drunk now she's losing her mind least I got her to stop drinking.
But I'm also terminally ill and going through hell and she's compounding it, I'd leave but I'm so fucking sick and poor I couldn't give 50 miles.

62311374
>>62311348
doesnt sound great at all. like im saying seek help in the hands of bachus....
(its all fantasy and bollocks dont take that for serious!)

62311420
>>62311374
I wish my life was a fantasy, I had a terrible surgery in April and for 10 days after i kept dreaming of a beautiful world similar to ours but no pain and nobody being hurtful just playful in harmless ways.
And the skyline was beautiful orange and blue clouds/skyline every day.
When I'd wake up I'd be physically reaching out as hard as I could.

62311518
>>62311420
i hope you find your female helping you

item.title: 65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC — 9 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
65K IS THE BOTTOM FOR BTC

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-03 20:56 UTC

OP

>disregard dwarves
>disregard doomerfags
>if you bought the rally, hold
>if you didn't, enjoy the discount

Bitcoin is a stateless, free, private currency that draws intrinsic value. You can buy groceries with BTC, you can't buy it with rock collections or shitcoins. And ever less the fiat bullshit can buy groceries.

>but BTC is dumping to zero--

Zoom out.

Bitcoin is the future of currency and if you haven't realized yet, you are either a fool, a scammer, or a paid shill.

Buy the fucking dip and stop selling the bottom.

‐ Yours truly, the BUYROG, pmetalsbane.

Early replies (first 3)

62309508
>>62309503
proof??

62309515
This thread now is the 64k waiting room

62309535
>>62309508
I don't need proof. I made money for everyone who bought the previous dip, who were considering cashing out and start a rock collection. And this time it isn't going to be any fucking different.

ETF returns are pathetic. PM is not investment. Bonds are tanking.

Buy BTC.

Let's get rich together.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62309559
It is so annoying that an entire asset class need to be defended by the financially illiterate, bear trolls and the mentally ill. Eveything is a suckers rally or a dead cat bounce.

You people talk so much about buying the dip. Then the dip happens, and you mock those who buy it.

Bearposting needs to be banned from the board

62309584
>>62309559
It seems Bitcoin is an asset, the purely image based crypto coins are the asset class.
And it seems it dipped to 60k a few weeks ago. These little spikes tend to be retested.

62310185
I had a dream where bitcoin went to $11k and then I woke up the next day and it had gone to $2k. I didn't consider buying it at those prices, I just felt happy that loads of people will have lost all their savings

62310319
>>62309535
omg

62311471
>>62309515
64k just in. Where can I find the next waiting room, good Sir?

62311487
>>62309503
thoughts?

item.title: Day 19 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000 — 3 replies — 1.3/hr

Day 19 of cyber-snailposting every day until ICP is at $1000

Open thread • Replies: 3 • Updated: 2026-06-03 19:27 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62308957

62310939
/pol/ brainlets btfo, ok? Moonman scam finally getting reveal

62311005
please fuck my ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 100 replies — 38.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 100 • Updated: 2026-06-03 19:10 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

62294595
>>62284814
Just buy the dip and sell the high simple. The main thing is to hold. I'm not only holding, I also staked some of my BTC recently since we can do that now without bridge involved.

62296711
BEHOLD, THE FUTURE

62296738
>>62296711

62297476
AAAAAAAAAAAAAA

62298184
>>62296711
Is that you Michael?

62298251
>>62284814
>buy signal
>5/28
This aged like milk.

62299491
>>62298251
>can't read
Maybe investing is not for you anon

>>62298184
Shhh it's a secret

62301262
>>62292563
>>62292730
what's a hyperliquid vault

62301440
>tfw if I listened to the schizo chart I would be down 10%

62301542
All i can see is that you've been incorrect? Am i missing something? Lol

62302104
>>62301542
>>62301440
We'll see - the hit rate's 78%, not 100%. The general sense, per the twitter thread yesterday, is bounce to the 5th-7th, then drop to the real bottom mid-June and then pump through summer, but obviously Brownian motion can't predict Saylor selling lol

62303698
Tell me no one bought because of this indicator please HAHAHAHAHA

62303805
Is this some intraday trading indicator? Like you were supposed to buy and then sell with leverage the day of the OP? If you would have bought and were holding now you would be fucked

62304332
reverse indicator of op is printing me money

62305278
Look how they massacred my coin

62305335
>>62296711
I appreciate the insight. This thread has been an interesting read.

62305363
>>62305335
Thanks Anon, wish it was going better right now though!
Liquidity's good, the sim says "increasing demand," sure enough stocks are up and copper/gold is acting like a boom is coming, but crypto is just getting slaughtered. The only explanation I can think of is we're getting fucked now that CME crypto futures are legal. But without any actual data that's probably just cope

62305385
it's over

62306031
>>62305363
I had an asset do 200% in like 7 days recently, so it seems at least that people aren't turning up their noses at it all, so long as substantial developments are being made. Still not immune to dipping after considering profit-taking. Good projects can stand out in a storm like a lighthouse does. We'll see if that continues into the year.

62306711
>>62306031
Oh, I don't think crypto's over at all, unless someone has cracked SHA-256. Something is definitely happening to the asset class, though. A pro-crypto Fed chair just took over, stocks are up, but our internet coins are acting like they're about to be banned (for the tenth time)

62308784
Bump

62308863
>>62285441
You can trade at mexc and make maker orders close to the current price, that way only the taker pays 0.4-5% and you lose nothing.

62309487
>>62306031
Consistency is key.

62310796
>>62308784
I think we're fully in a news-driven cycle now, or maybe more accurately a institutional-crime-driven cycle. Physics-liquidity has no edge rn.
I'm working on this seasonality model which has caught some pivots so far, and if it's right we trend down to mid-June and then pump hard through the summer, but it's extremely experimental and I'll need months more results to compare to predictions before I could even start tuning it

62310807
>>62310796
it could also be the observer effect has broken your model

62310837
>>62310807
>the observer effect has broken your model
If Saylor's found my model he could at least send me a couple shekels
That said, about 95 times out of 100 a physics-predicted pivot that happens on a weekend gets frontrun the last trading day before, which does make me suspect institutions also figured this out

62310897
>>62285204
>the thing has a ~80% hit rate over more than a year

80% of the time it works all of the time

62310926
based

item.title: HOLY. FUCK. — 44 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
HOLY. FUCK.

Open thread • Replies: 44 • Updated: 2026-06-03 16:47 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62301820
@grok is this legal?

62301873
>>62301796
iirc I don't think the wash sale rule applies to crypto yet, so yes you can if that is true. You might get in trouble if your dealing with a significant sum though or like sell enough to deduct your entire taxable income and your job isn't a Mcwage job, the IRS has a catch-22 called the economic substance doctrine where they could argue that you were trying to fleece them in an audit or court proceeding.

62301881
There are actively managed funds that intentionally do stuff like this to get you tax breaks every year.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62303270

Wrong, I've taken the max deduction every single year for the past 5 years because I blow at trading

62303329
>>62301796
You can do this assuming the investment isn’t subject to wash sale rules. You can also swap between bitcoin ETFs that aren’t considered substantially identical. However doing this is going to make your cost basis much lower so when you sell in the future at a profit, you’ll have higher levels of taxable gains.

62303351
>>62303255
You can harvest unlimited losses to offset capital gains. If you sell stock A at a 5 mil profit and sell stock B at a 3 mil loss, you’ll only have 2 mil in taxable gains that year. The 3k limit only applies to lowering MAGI.

62303503
>people on /biz/ dont understand how capital gains taxes work
jesus christ guys get it together

62303568
>>62303270
based

62303876
>>62301905
There is no wash sales on crypto because its not a security, its classified as "property". That said its still not good to take losses. Making 10k is always better than losing 10k even if it lets you defer taxes on other earnings.

62303887
>>62303351
It has to be the same type of capital loss and gains though. You can't offset short term loss against long term gains.

62303938
>>62302190
correct, and you carry over anything not used to the next year

62304653
>>62303887
Look how confident this utter retard is. Lot of retards in here though excluding a couple anons.
It initially applies directly to the same type of capital gain/loss (short/long term). I’ll just give an example cause it’s harder to type out and explain it simply.

let’s say you
>realize a loss of 15k in a short term trade in Feb of 2026
>in April, you close another short term trade and realize a gain of $2500 in April.
>That puts you at -12500 on the year so far. Short for short, straightforward and as expected.
>You make no more sales of short term holdings for the rest of the year.
>in November, you sell a long term position and profit 9500 ltcg.
->since you have no more short term gains to cancel out, you can apply it to your long term gain
>you would end up net -3k on the year which you can then deduct from you MAGI
And ideally if you’re really scheming and planning, you obviously don’t want to realize a long term capital gain to cut down on a realized short term loss. It’s possible in incorrect about that last sentence. I won’t be smug like the brainlet I replied to. From my understanding you can’t just defer that long term realized gain

Here’s somethin I was thinking about as a possible plan I was contemplating (although I am aware about it carrying forward and I could use my “loss Bank” later in the future) , I have 19k in realized losses short from being a Stink marine already this year. I have long term precious metals, which are taxed at a rate of 28% on long term holds. I could realize my profits and sell some early gold bars that I got for low 2k as well as silver at 30, and use those short term losses to completely cancel out my realized profits in PMs 1:1.
